title: CLIs with argparse
subtitle: From args[] parsing to a real command-line tool

sections.java: // Hand-rolled args[] parsing public static void main(String[] args) { String path = args[0]; int limit = Integer.parseInt(args[1]); // no --help, no validation, no defaults // (or pull in picocli for annotations) }
sections.python: # argparse: declarative, stdlib, batteries included import argparse p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Load rows") p.add_argument("path") # positional p.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=100) p.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true") args = p.parse_args() run(args.path, args.limit)

sections.title: --help, Types, and Validation for Free
sections.content: Declaring arguments gives you an auto-generated --help screen, type conversion (type=int), defaults, required flags, choices (choices=[...]), and clear usage errors — all from the stdlib, no dependency. action="store_true" makes a boolean flag. It's the picocli experience without adding a library.

sections.java: // Subcommands need a framework (picocli) @Command(name = "db", subcommands = { Migrate.class, Seed.class }) class Db { }
sections.python: # Subcommands via add_subparsers (git-style) sub = p.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True) migrate = sub.add_parser("migrate") seed = sub.add_parser("seed") seed.add_argument("--rows", type=int) # dispatch on args.cmd

sections.title: When to Reach Beyond the Stdlib
sections.content: argparse covers most tools, but for richer ergonomics the ecosystem favors typer (build a CLI from type-hinted function signatures — very FastAPI-like) or click (decorator-based). Pattern: prototype with argparse, graduate to typer when you want subcommands, shell completion, and less boilerplate. All read args from sys.argv under the hood.

sections.question: What does add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true") create?
options: A required string option named verbose
A boolean flag: present → True, absent → False
An integer counter of how many times it appears
sections.explanation: action="store_true" defines a boolean flag — passing --verbose sets args.verbose to True, omitting it leaves False. No value is consumed after it. (For a repeat-count flag like -vvv you'd use action="count".)
